mechanism_narrative: Quarterly real GDP growth exceeding the 2.5% annualized trend compresses equity risk premiums by confirming a durable post-recession recovery, driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in quarters with positive GDP acceleration are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A monthly decline in the unemployment rate signals tightening labor markets that boost household disposable income and consumption demand, leading to positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces negative 20-day returns in > 60% of instances where UNRATE drops month-over-month, or the signal's predictive power vanishes when controlling for FEDFUNDS changes.

mechanism_narrative: The joint behavior of falling federal funds rates and stable consumer prices reduces real borrowing costs without triggering inflation fears, compressing discount rates and lifting equity valuations over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: The interaction term (FEDFUNDS decline × CPIAUCSL stability) shows a negative or statistically insignificant coefficient in a multivariate regression of 60-day forward returns over a 3-year out-of-sample period.

mechanism_narrative: Rising industrial production indicates expanding manufacturing capacity and strong corporate order books, which translate into higher expected earnings and positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: 5-day forward returns are uncorrelated with month-over-month INDPRO changes (correlation < 0.05), or INDPRO rises while SPX falls in more than 50% of observed months.

mechanism_narrative: Sustained growth in housing starts reflects improving consumer confidence and credit availability, driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days as construction and related sectors boost aggregate demand.
failure_condition: HOUST month-over-month growth shows no predictive relationship with 20-day SPX returns (Rank IC < 0.02), or housing booms consistently precede equity drawdowns due to rate hike expectations.

mechanism_narrative: An accelerating pace of federal funds rate hikes signals aggressive monetary tightening that increases discount rates and dampens equity valuations, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX generates positive 20-day returns in > 60% of triggered instances despite the rate acceleration, or the signal's negative predictive power disappears when GDP growth exceeds 3%.

mechanism_narrative: The simultaneous expansion of nonfarm payrolls and commercial/industrial loan balances indicates synchronized growth in labor demand and corporate credit access, fueling earnings upgrades and positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: The joint signal of rising PAYEMS and RSAFS fails to outperform a baseline buy-and-hold strategy over a 3-year walk-forward test, or the interaction coefficient becomes insignificant when controlling for INDPRO.

mechanism_narrative: Diverging trends where real GDP growth stagnates while consumer prices accelerate compress corporate profit margins and trigger valuation multiple contraction, leading to negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Equity markets rally despite simultaneous GDP stagnation and CPI acceleration, or the divergence signal yields a positive Rank IC over any 2-year rolling window.
